title: Novavax Q1 2023 Earnings: Seasonal Demand and Significant Revenue Decline Year-Over-Year
text: Due to an emerging seasonal demand pattern for COVID vaccines, total revenue for the first quarter of 2023 was $81 million compared to $704 million in the same period in 2022. Novavax reported a net loss of $294 million, or $3.41 per share, compared with net income of $203 million, or $2.56 per share, during the first quarter of 2022. The dramatic year-over-year revenue decline of 88% highlighted the challenges facing Novavax as the COVID vaccine market matured and demand became more seasonal. Novavax forecasted 2023 sales of $1.4 billion to $1.6 billion in its first-quarter earnings report. This guidance proved too optimistic and was later revised downward multiple times throughout 2023. The company's transition from pandemic-era revenues to normalized market conditions proved more challenging than initially expected. The shift from consistent quarterly revenues during the height of the pandemic to seasonal patterns required significant operational adjustments and strategic reassessment.
